meetings.title: Timeco Quick Overview
meetings.summary:
✅ System Configuration Finalized: Finalized key system settings for Florida and Indianapolis, ensuring clarity on PTO and bereavement leave types for employees.
🌐 VPN and IP Whitelisting Introduced: Improved remote access by requiring employee-provided IP addresses for smoother login.
⏰ Overtime Calculation Automated: Automated overtime calculations for hourly employees; salaried employees' overtime is not visible to avoid confusion.
📑 Expense Approvals Defined: Expense approvals must occur within the current pay period, ensuring efficient submissions management for managers.
👥 All Employees Needed in TimeCo: All employees, except contractors, must be included in TimeCo for accurate timesheet management.
🧹 Data Cleanup Essential: Data cleanup and role assignments are essential before system launch, with testing planned for workflow verification.

meetings.title: Review Superintendent SOP
meetings.summary: The team discussed the implementation of Scribe to create video tutorials aimed at improving training and standard operating procedures, with a particular focus on daily reporting to enhance documentation accuracy. Jesse Dawson highlighted the need for detailed logs on crucial tasks, while Parker Hollingsworth will produce videos demonstrating processes like schedule management. The meeting also addressed the shift to a Monday-Friday work schedule to manage workloads across multiple sites more effectively, alongside established protocols for equipment deliveries to ensure safety and efficiency. Bidding oversight was improved by gaining access to Building Connected for tracking proposals and following up with subcontractors. Overall, the team's commitment to improved communication and operational efficiency was emphasized throughout the discussion.

meetings.title: Estimating Meeting | Enzo A. & Jake R.
meetings.summary: The team discussed urgent strategies to secure bids by April 18, with a Friday cutoff for submissions. Enzo highlighted the need for persistent vendor follow-ups to obtain three bids per division, as Jake reported progress on securing two bidders per division while nearing completion on outreach for most trades. The meeting emphasized using the CSI MasterFormat for clarity in project divisions and included the addition of new divisions to ensure comprehensive bid coverage. Furthermore, Victor's role transition to support ongoing projects in Illinois was discussed, ensuring no gaps in outreach. Overall, the meeting aimed at maximizing bid commitments through effective communication and collaboration.

meetings.title: Mezzanine
meetings.summary: The team focused on optimizing structural design to reduce costs by potentially removing perimeter rails and columns, as suggested by Keith Fisher. This could simplify the design, subsequently lowering material and labor expenses. Coordination was emphasized to prepare revised drawings and pricing ahead of a critical meeting at 1:00 PM, with Bob Wright stressing the need for timely decisions, especially regarding the lead times for beams. Beams presented a delivery risk of up to six weeks, while columns were readily available. The discussion also highlighted the fabrication schedule starting mid-May and targeted erection by mid-June. Rising material costs due to market conditions were acknowledged, prompting the need for careful budget management and transparency with the owner regarding potential increases.

meetings.title: FIT Volt - Certification
meetings.summary: The team deliberated on the certification strategy for solar roof systems, deciding to pursue Intertek for certification due to its acceptance of Fraunhofer test data, thus saving time and costs. Intertek's European and Florida labs are experienced, making it a faster option compared to CSA, which has limited European responsiveness. UL certification was deemed too slow and costly, leading to a preference for system-level certification rather than individual components. They also confirmed that gutter systems generally do not require certification, relying instead on local approvals. Rapid shutdown devices were identified as essential for enhancing firefighter safety in US installations. Weekly meetings were arranged for effective coordination, with a target certification timeline of approximately three months.

meetings.title: CEO Advisor :: Brandon & Rob
meetings.summary: Participants discussed the shortcomings of the recent Tony Robbins Lions event, noting poor content quality and a lack of engaging experiences, leading to attendee dissatisfaction. Financially, the company reported a loss of $60,000 for the quarter while maintaining ambitious annual revenue goals of $60 million. Concerns were raised about decreasing gross margins, driven by rising subcontractor costs and excessive marketing expenditures. The necessity for a formal budget was highlighted as a way to improve financial management and operational discipline. Additionally, the potential departure of key personnel, particularly project manager Jesse, was discussed, along with strategies to retain talent through equity options. The company’s high debt-to-equity ratio was also emphasized, indicating urgent attention is needed for financial restructuring.

Jesse Dawson highlighted the need for detailed logs on crucial tasks, while Parker Hollingsworth will produce videos demonstrating processes like schedule management. meetings.title: Estimating Meeting | Enzo A. & Jake R.
meetings.summary: The team discussed urgent strategies to secure bids by April 18, with a Friday cutoff for submissions. Enzo highlighted the need for persistent vendor follow-ups to obtain three bids per division, as Jake reported progress on securing two bidders per division while nearing completion on outreach for most trades. The meeting emphasized using the CSI MasterFormat for clarity in project divisions and included the addition of new divisions to ensure comprehensive bid coverage. Furthermore, Victor's role transition to support ongoing projects in Illinois was discussed, ensuring no gaps in outreach. Overall, the meeting aimed at maximizing bid commitments through effective communication and collaboration.

meetings.title: Mezzanine
meetings.summary: The team focused on optimizing structural design to reduce costs by potentially removing perimeter rails and columns, as suggested by Keith Fisher. This could simplify the design, subsequently lowering material and labor expenses. Coordination was emphasized to prepare revised drawings and pricing ahead of a critical meeting at 1:00 PM, with Bob Wright stressing the need for timely decisions, especially regarding the lead times for beams. Beams presented a delivery risk of up to six weeks, while columns were readily available. The discussion also highlighted the fabrication schedule starting mid-May and targeted erection by mid-June. Rising material costs due to market conditions were acknowledged, prompting the need for careful budget management and transparency with the owner regarding potential increases.

meetings.title: FIT Volt - Certification
meetings.summary: The team deliberated on the certification strategy for solar roof systems, deciding to pursue Intertek for certification due to its acceptance of Fraunhofer test data, thus saving time and costs. Intertek's European and Florida labs are experienced, making it a faster option compared to CSA, which has limited European responsiveness. UL certification was deemed too slow and costly, leading to a preference for system-level certification rather than individual components. They also confirmed that gutter systems generally do not require certification, relying instead on local approvals. Rapid shutdown devices were identified as essential for enhancing firefighter safety in US installations. Weekly meetings were arranged for effective coordination, with a target certification timeline of approximately three months.

meetings.title: CEO Advisor :: Brandon & Rob
meetings.summary: Participants discussed the shortcomings of the recent Tony Robbins Lions event, noting poor content quality and a lack of engaging experiences, leading to attendee dissatisfaction. Financially, the company reported a loss of $60,000 for the quarter while maintaining ambitious annual revenue goals of $60 million. Concerns were raised about decreasing gross margins, driven by rising subcontractor costs and excessive marketing expenditures. The necessity for a formal budget was highlighted as a way to improve financial management and operational discipline. Additionally, the potential departure of key personnel, particularly project manager Jesse, was discussed, along with strategies to retain talent through equity options. The company’s high debt-to-equity ratio was also emphasized, indicating urgent attention is needed for financial restructuring.

meetings.title: Mezzanine

Rising material costs due to market conditions were acknowledged, prompting the need for careful budget management and transparency with the owner regarding potential increases.

Rapid shutdown devices were identified as essential for enhancing firefighter safety in US installations. meetings.sourceRef: [Source: Meeting - "Estimating Meeting | Enzo A. & Jake R." - 2026-05-13T13:00:00+00:00]
meetings.title: Estimating Meeting | Enzo A. & Jake R.
meetings.summary: The team discussed urgent strategies to secure bids by April 18, with a Friday cutoff for submissions. Enzo highlighted the need for persistent vendor follow-ups to obtain three bids per division, as Jake reported progress on securing two bidders per division while nearing completion on outreach for most trades. The meeting emphasized using the CSI MasterFormat for clarity in project divisions and included the addition of new divisions to ensure comprehensive bid coverage. Furthermore, Victor's role transition to support ongoing projects in Illinois was discussed, ensuring no gaps in outreach. Overall, the meeting aimed at maximizing bid commitments through effective communication and collaboration.

meetings.title: Mezzanine
meetings.summary: The team focused on optimizing structural design to reduce costs by potentially removing perimeter rails and columns, as suggested by Keith Fisher. This could simplify the design, subsequently lowering material and labor expenses. Coordination was emphasized to prepare revised drawings and pricing ahead of a critical meeting at 1:00 PM, with Bob Wright stressing the need for timely decisions, especially regarding the lead times for beams. Beams presented a delivery risk of up to six weeks, while columns were readily available. The discussion also highlighted the fabrication schedule starting mid-May and targeted erection by mid-June. Rising material costs due to market conditions were acknowledged, prompting the need for careful budget management and transparency with the owner regarding potential increases.

meetings.title: FIT Volt - Certification
meetings.summary: The team deliberated on the certification strategy for solar roof systems, deciding to pursue Intertek for certification due to its acceptance of Fraunhofer test data, thus saving time and costs. Intertek's European and Florida labs are experienced, making it a faster option compared to CSA, which has limited European responsiveness. UL certification was deemed too slow and costly, leading to a preference for system-level certification rather than individual components. They also confirmed that gutter systems generally do not require certification, relying instead on local approvals. Rapid shutdown devices were identified as essential for enhancing firefighter safety in US installations. Weekly meetings were arranged for effective coordination, with a target certification timeline of approximately three months.

meetings.title: CEO Advisor :: Brandon & Rob
meetings.summary: Participants discussed the shortcomings of the recent Tony Robbins Lions event, noting poor content quality and a lack of engaging experiences, leading to attendee dissatisfaction. Financially, the company reported a loss of $60,000 for the quarter while maintaining ambitious annual revenue goals of $60 million. Concerns were raised about decreasing gross margins, driven by rising subcontractor costs and excessive marketing expenditures. The necessity for a formal budget was highlighted as a way to improve financial management and operational discipline. Additionally, the potential departure of key personnel, particularly project manager Jesse, was discussed, along with strategies to retain talent through equity options. The company’s high debt-to-equity ratio was also emphasized, indicating urgent attention is needed for financial restructuring.

meetings.summary: The team discussed urgent strategies to secure bids by April 18, with a Friday cutoff for submissions. The meeting emphasized using the CSI MasterFormat for clarity in project divisions and included the addition of new divisions to ensure comprehensive bid coverage. Furthermore, Victor's role transition to support ongoing projects in Illinois was discussed, ensuring no gaps in outreach. Overall, the meeting aimed at maximizing bid commitments through effective communication and collaboration.

meetings.title: Mezzanine
meetings.summary: The team focused on optimizing structural design to reduce costs by potentially removing perimeter rails and columns, as suggested by Keith Fisher. This could simplify the design, subsequently lowering material and labor expenses. Coordination was emphasized to prepare revised drawings and pricing ahead of a critical meeting at 1:00 PM, with Bob Wright stressing the need for timely decisions, especially regarding the lead times for beams. Beams presented a delivery risk of up to six weeks, while columns were readily available. The discussion also highlighted the fabrication schedule starting mid-May and targeted erection by mid-June. Rising material costs due to market conditions were acknowledged, prompting the need for careful budget management and transparency with the owner regarding potential increases.

meetings.title: FIT Volt - Certification
meetings.summary: The team deliberated on the certification strategy for solar roof systems, deciding to pursue Intertek for certification due to its acceptance of Fraunhofer test data, thus saving time and costs. Intertek's European and Florida labs are experienced, making it a faster option compared to CSA, which has limited European responsiveness. UL certification was deemed too slow and costly, leading to a preference for system-level certification rather than individual components. They also confirmed that gutter systems generally do not require certification, relying instead on local approvals. Rapid shutdown devices were identified as essential for enhancing firefighter safety in US installations. Weekly meetings were arranged for effective coordination, with a target certification timeline of approximately three months.

meetings.title: Review Superintendent SOP
meetings.summary: The team discussed the implementation of Scribe to create video tutorials aimed at improving training and standard operating procedures, with a particular focus on daily reporting to enhance documentation accuracy. Jesse Dawson highlighted the need for detailed logs on crucial tasks, while Parker Hollingsworth will produce videos demonstrating processes like schedule management. The meeting also addressed the shift to a Monday-Friday work schedule to manage workloads across multiple sites more effectively, alongside established protocols for equipment deliveries to ensure safety and efficiency. Bidding oversight was improved by gaining access to Building Connected for tracking proposals and following up with subcontractors. Overall, the team's commitment to improved communication and operational efficiency was emphasized throughout the discussion.

meetings.title: Estimating Meeting | Enzo A. & Jake R.
meetings.summary: The team discussed urgent strategies to secure bids by April 18, with a Friday cutoff for submissions. Enzo highlighted the need for persistent vendor follow-ups to obtain three bids per division, as Jake reported progress on securing two bidders per division while nearing completion on outreach for most trades. The meeting emphasized using the CSI MasterFormat for clarity in project divisions and included the addition of new divisions to ensure comprehensive bid coverage. Furthermore, Victor's role transition to support ongoing projects in Illinois was discussed, ensuring no gaps in outreach. Overall, the meeting aimed at maximizing bid commitments through effective communication and collaboration.

meetings.title: Mezzanine
meetings.summary: The team focused on optimizing structural design to reduce costs by potentially removing perimeter rails and columns, as suggested by Keith Fisher. This could simplify the design, subsequently lowering material and labor expenses. Coordination was emphasized to prepare revised drawings and pricing ahead of a critical meeting at 1:00 PM, with Bob Wright stressing the need for timely decisions, especially regarding the lead times for beams. Beams presented a delivery risk of up to six weeks, while columns were readily available. The discussion also highlighted the fabrication schedule starting mid-May and targeted erection by mid-June. Rising material costs due to market conditions were acknowledged, prompting the need for careful budget management and transparency with the owner regarding potential increases.

meetings.title: FIT Volt - Certification
meetings.summary: The team deliberated on the certification strategy for solar roof systems, deciding to pursue Intertek for certification due to its acceptance of Fraunhofer test data, thus saving time and costs. Intertek's European and Florida labs are experienced, making it a faster option compared to CSA, which has limited European responsiveness. UL certification was deemed too slow and costly, leading to a preference for system-level certification rather than individual components. They also confirmed that gutter systems generally do not require certification, relying instead on local approvals. Rapid shutdown devices were identified as essential for enhancing firefighter safety in US installations. Weekly meetings were arranged for effective coordination, with a target certification timeline of approximately three months.

meetings.title: CEO Advisor :: Brandon & Rob
meetings.summary: Participants discussed the shortcomings of the recent Tony Robbins Lions event, noting poor content quality and a lack of engaging experiences, leading to attendee dissatisfaction. Financially, the company reported a loss of $60,000 for the quarter while maintaining ambitious annual revenue goals of $60 million. Concerns were raised about decreasing gross margins, driven by rising subcontractor costs and excessive marketing expenditures. The necessity for a formal budget was highlighted as a way to improve financial management and operational discipline. Additionally, the potential departure of key personnel, particularly project manager Jesse, was discussed, along with strategies to retain talent through equity options. The company’s high debt-to-equity ratio was also emphasized, indicating urgent attention is needed for financial restructuring.

meetings.title: Mezzanine
meetings.summary: The team focused on optimizing structural design to reduce costs by potentially removing perimeter rails and columns, as suggested by Keith Fisher. This could simplify the design, subsequently lowering material and labor expenses. Coordination was emphasized to prepare revised drawings and pricing ahead of a critical meeting at 1:00 PM, with Bob Wright stressing the need for timely decisions, especially regarding the lead times for beams. Beams presented a delivery risk of up to six weeks, while columns were readily available. The discussion also highlighted the fabrication schedule starting mid-May and targeted erection by mid-June. Rising material costs due to market conditions were acknowledged, prompting the need for careful budget management and transparency with the owner regarding potential increases.

meetings.title: FIT Volt - Certification
meetings.summary: The team deliberated on the certification strategy for solar roof systems, deciding to pursue Intertek for certification due to its acceptance of Fraunhofer test data, thus saving time and costs. Intertek's European and Florida labs are experienced, making it a faster option compared to CSA, which has limited European responsiveness. UL certification was deemed too slow and costly, leading to a preference for system-level certification rather than individual components. They also confirmed that gutter systems generally do not require certification, relying instead on local approvals. Rapid shutdown devices were identified as essential for enhancing firefighter safety in US installations. Weekly meetings were arranged for effective coordination, with a target certification timeline of approximately three months.

meetings.title: CEO Advisor :: Brandon & Rob
meetings.summary: Participants discussed the shortcomings of the recent Tony Robbins Lions event, noting poor content quality and a lack of engaging experiences, leading to attendee dissatisfaction. Financially, the company reported a loss of $60,000 for the quarter while maintaining ambitious annual revenue goals of $60 million. Concerns were raised about decreasing gross margins, driven by rising subcontractor costs and excessive marketing expenditures. The necessity for a formal budget was highlighted as a way to improve financial management and operational discipline. Additionally, the potential departure of key personnel, particularly project manager Jesse, was discussed, along with strategies to retain talent through equity options. The company’s high debt-to-equity ratio was also emphasized, indicating urgent attention is needed for financial restructuring.

meetings.title: Review Superintendent SOP
meetings.summary: The team discussed the implementation of Scribe to create video tutorials aimed at improving training and standard operating procedures, with a particular focus on daily reporting to enhance documentation accuracy. Jesse Dawson highlighted the need for detailed logs on crucial tasks, while Parker Hollingsworth will produce videos demonstrating processes like schedule management. The meeting also addressed the shift to a Monday-Friday work schedule to manage workloads across multiple sites more effectively, alongside established protocols for equipment deliveries to ensure safety and efficiency. Bidding oversight was improved by gaining access to Building Connected for tracking proposals and following up with subcontractors. Overall, the team's commitment to improved communication and operational efficiency was emphasized throughout the discussion.

meetings.title: Estimating Meeting | Enzo A. & Jake R.
meetings.summary: The team discussed urgent strategies to secure bids by April 18, with a Friday cutoff for submissions. Enzo highlighted the need for persistent vendor follow-ups to obtain three bids per division, as Jake reported progress on securing two bidders per division while nearing completion on outreach for most trades. The meeting emphasized using the CSI MasterFormat for clarity in project divisions and included the addition of new divisions to ensure comprehensive bid coverage. Furthermore, Victor's role transition to support ongoing projects in Illinois was discussed, ensuring no gaps in outreach. Overall, the meeting aimed at maximizing bid commitments through effective communication and collaboration.

meetings.title: Mezzanine
meetings.summary: The team focused on optimizing structural design to reduce costs by potentially removing perimeter rails and columns, as suggested by Keith Fisher. This could simplify the design, subsequently lowering material and labor expenses. Coordination was emphasized to prepare revised drawings and pricing ahead of a critical meeting at 1:00 PM, with Bob Wright stressing the need for timely decisions, especially regarding the lead times for beams. Beams presented a delivery risk of up to six weeks, while columns were readily available. The discussion also highlighted the fabrication schedule starting mid-May and targeted erection by mid-June. Rising material costs due to market conditions were acknowledged, prompting the need for careful budget management and transparency with the owner regarding potential increases.

meetings.title: FIT Volt - Certification
meetings.summary: The team deliberated on the certification strategy for solar roof systems, deciding to pursue Intertek for certification due to its acceptance of Fraunhofer test data, thus saving time and costs. Intertek's European and Florida labs are experienced, making it a faster option compared to CSA, which has limited European responsiveness. UL certification was deemed too slow and costly, leading to a preference for system-level certification rather than individual components. They also confirmed that gutter systems generally do not require certification, relying instead on local approvals. Rapid shutdown devices were identified as essential for enhancing firefighter safety in US installations. Weekly meetings were arranged for effective coordination, with a target certification timeline of approximately three months.

meetings.title: CEO Advisor :: Brandon & Rob
meetings.summary: Participants discussed the shortcomings of the recent Tony Robbins Lions event, noting poor content quality and a lack of engaging experiences, leading to attendee dissatisfaction. Financially, the company reported a loss of $60,000 for the quarter while maintaining ambitious annual revenue goals of $60 million. Concerns were raised about decreasing gross margins, driven by rising subcontractor costs and excessive marketing expenditures. The necessity for a formal budget was highlighted as a way to improve financial management and operational discipline. Additionally, the potential departure of key personnel, particularly project manager Jesse, was discussed, along with strategies to retain talent through equity options. The company’s high debt-to-equity ratio was also emphasized, indicating urgent attention is needed for financial restructuring.

This could simplify the design, subsequently lowering material and labor expenses. Beams presented a delivery risk of up to six weeks, while columns were readily available. The discussion also highlighted the fabrication schedule starting mid-May and targeted erection by mid-June. meetings.title: FIT Volt - Certification
meetings.summary: The team deliberated on the certification strategy for solar roof systems, deciding to pursue Intertek for certification due to its acceptance of Fraunhofer test data, thus saving time and costs. Intertek's European and Florida labs are experienced, making it a faster option compared to CSA, which has limited European responsiveness. UL certification was deemed too slow and costly, leading to a preference for system-level certification rather than individual components. They also confirmed that gutter systems generally do not require certification, relying instead on local approvals. Rapid shutdown devices were identified as essential for enhancing firefighter safety in US installations. Weekly meetings were arranged for effective coordination, with a target certification timeline of approximately three months.

steps.label: Intent and project scope
steps.whenUsed: Every turn. The assistant classifies the request, applies selected project scope, and decides whether the answer needs packet, structured, source, or write-action handling.
steps.failureMode: If the request is ambiguous, AA should ask for the missing project or action detail instead of guessing.
steps.sourceOfTruth: intent router, selectedProjectId, project access guardrails

steps.label: Current intelligence packet
steps.whenUsed: Project status, risk, catch-up, owner-readout, and strategic questions use the current packet first when a fresh packet exists.
steps.failureMode: If the packet is missing or stale, AA must say that and continue with structured/source retrieval.

steps.label: Project operating snapshot
steps.whenUsed: Used when the question needs structured current-state data such as project controls, tasks, financials, and operating-summary source coverage.
steps.failureMode: If a source category is unavailable, AA should surface the gap rather than treating the snapshot as complete.
steps.sourceOfTruth: project briefing snapshot and operating-summary services

steps.label: Structured tools
steps.whenUsed: Used for precise totals, counts, IDs, statuses, ownership, and workflow records.
steps.failureMode: If a query fails, AA should return the tool-error envelope and explain which source failed.
steps.sourceOfTruth: Supabase tables/views and scoped project tools

steps.label: Source-specific retrieval
steps.whenUsed: Used for meetings, emails, Teams messages, documents, app help, and questions where the answer lives in unstructured communications.
steps.failureMode: If a source is empty or timed out, AA should cite the attempted source and answer from remaining evidence only.

steps.label: Specialist or action tools
steps.whenUsed: Used after evidence is scoped when a CFO/COO/CRO/CHRO/VPBD/CMO opinion or preview-first write workflow is needed.
steps.failureMode: Writes must stay preview-first unless the tool has explicit confirmation and idempotency protection.
steps.sourceOfTruth: C-suite orchestrator, action tools, feature request tools

steps.label: Answer, citations, and trace
steps.whenUsed: Every turn. AA persists tool traces, provider decision, loop diagnostic, source health, quality score, and retrieval policy metadata to chat history.
steps.failureMode: If metadata cannot be persisted, the request should fail loudly instead of presenting observability as complete.

assistant_retrieval_order.summary: AA scopes the question first, prefers fresh compiled project intelligence for strategic questions, layers structured project data, then uses source-specific retrieval and specialist/action tools as needed.
